tasks.description: Phase 1 comments and cleanup (Steps 1-4). (a) In AgentChat.tsx around line 38, add a comment to mergeSelectedClips explaining why gallery clips are preferred over timeline clips (they carry generationId which timeline clips may lack for the same URL). (b) In GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx lines 180-185, replace the two inline arrow function props (onAddToLastShot, onAddToShotWithoutPosition) with direct references to gallery.onAddToShot / gallery.onAddToShotWithoutPosition — these are pure pass-throughs. (c) In shotCreationPaths.ts above the serial loop around line 117, add a comment: the add_generation_to_shot RPC computes timeline_frame from MAX(shot_generations.timeline_frame) + 50 without row-level locking, so parallel calls would race and assign duplicate positions — serial insertion is required. Do NOT change the loop structure. (d) In shotCreationTypes.ts, delete the unused type aliases ShotGenerationPreview (line 144) and ShotGenerationRow (line 145) — verify they are not imported anywhere first.
tasks.executor_notes: Added the requested explanatory comments, replaced the two gallery shot handler wrappers with direct function references after verifying the model uses the same MediaGallery handler type, and removed the unused shot-creation aliases after repo-wide grep showed no imports outside the target file.

tasks.description: Step 5: Simplify hasDataTransferType in dragDrop.ts (lines 40-81). All evergreen browsers support .includes() on DataTransfer.types (spec ReadonlyArray<string> since 2017). The DOMStringList path is legacy Chrome < 72. Replace the 4-path function with a 2-path version: try .includes() first, fall back to manual iteration for any edge case. Remove the DataTransferTypesList interface if it exists. Run dragDrop.test.ts after to confirm nothing breaks.
tasks.executor_notes: Simplified hasDataTransferType to the requested includes-first plus indexed-fallback shape while preserving the null/undefined guard for external drags. The dragDrop test file passed unchanged.

tasks.description: Step 6: Extract ClipAction context menu into a ClipContextMenu component. In ClipAction.tsx, extract the context menu portal (~lines 199-331, ~130 lines of conditional JSX) into a separate ClipContextMenu component defined in the same file. Keep the same props interface — goal is splitting visual clip rendering from menu rendering, not changing behavior. Total file should stay under 350 lines. FLAG-002 note: ClipAction.test.tsx exists and tests the create-shot and generate-video menu items — must be run after this change.
tasks.executor_notes: Extracted the portal/menu JSX into ClipContextMenu in the same file, kept the portal target at document.body, threaded all menu state through explicit props, and kept the file at 306 lines. ClipAction.test.tsx passed after the extraction.

tasks.description: Step 7: Add re-render bailout to useModifierKeys (useModifierKeys.ts lines 21-28). Use a ref to store previous computed values and only call setState when the new values differ from current state, avoiding unnecessary re-renders on every keydown/keyup. Run useModifierKeys.test.tsx after.
tasks.executor_notes: Added a ref-based field-by-field bailout over all modifier flags, kept the initial render state from useState(EMPTY_STATE), and avoided any ref/state feedback loop. The hook test file passed after the change.

tasks.description: Steps 8-9: Run ALL affected test files and review diff. Run these test files: GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx, SelectionContextMenu.test.tsx, shotCreationAction.test.ts, shotCreationPaths.test.ts, dragDrop.test.ts, useLassoSelection.test.tsx, useModifierKeys.test.tsx, useExternalDrop.test.tsx, GenerationsPaneGallery.test.tsx, MediaGalleryItem.behavior.test.tsx, AND ClipAction.test.tsx (per FLAG-002 — this file tests the exact menu surface extracted in T3). Fix any failures. Then review git diff to verify: (a) no out-of-scope changes, (b) no behavioral changes to selection/context-menu/shot-creation/drag-drop, (c) no new files created, (d) no new abstractions/helpers/utility files.

watch_items: FLAG-002 (open/minor): ClipAction.test.tsx exists and tests the context menu items being extracted in T3. Must be included in the test run even though the original plan Step 8 omitted it.
DEBT: Do not worsen agent-chat-rendering, agent-chat-undo-integration, or conflict-resolution debt items — these touch adjacent systems.

GenerationsPaneGallery inline arrow removal (T1b): verify the replaced references have the exact same signature. If gallery.onAddToShot takes different args than the inline wrapper, the wrapper is not a pure pass-through and should be kept.
shotCreationTypes.ts unused type deletion (T1d): grep for ShotGenerationPreview and ShotGenerationRow across the entire codebase before deleting — they may be imported from other files.
hasDataTransferType simplification (T2): the test file mocks DataTransfer.types — ensure the simplified code still works with the test's mock shape.
ClipAction extraction (T3): the context menu portal likely references state/callbacks from the parent component. Pass them as props to ClipContextMenu — don't use context or module-level state.
useModifierKeys bailout (T4): use a ref (not state) for the previous-value comparison to avoid infinite render loops. Compare individual boolean fields, not object reference equality.
No behavioral changes allowed — all changes are structural/comments only. If any test fails, the change likely introduced a behavioral regression.

Assumption: DOMStringList fallback is safe to remove (Chrome < 72 EOL). If dragDrop tests fail on the simplified version, the mock may simulate the old DOMStringList interface — update the test mock, not the production code.

sense_checks.question: Are the comments accurate and non-misleading? Is the GenerationsPaneGallery arrow function removal actually a pure pass-through (same args, same return)? Are ShotGenerationPreview/ShotGenerationRow truly unused across the entire codebase?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The AgentChat and shot-creation comments match the current logic, the GenerationsPaneGallery replacement is a true pass-through because the gallery model uses the same MediaGallery handler signature, and repo-wide grep found no remaining uses of ShotGenerationPreview or ShotGenerationRow in shotCreationTypes.

sense_checks.question: Does the simplified hasDataTransferType still handle the case where DataTransfer.types is undefined/null (drag events from external apps)? Did we remove exactly 2 of the 4 paths, keeping .includes() and manual iteration?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. hasDataTransferType still returns false when dataTransfer.types is missing, and the implementation now keeps exactly two paths: types.includes() first, then manual indexed iteration.

sense_checks.question: Does ClipContextMenu receive all needed state as props (no implicit closure over parent state)? Does the file stay under 350 lines total? Does the rendered output remain identical (same DOM structure, same portal target)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. ClipContextMenu gets its state and callbacks explicitly via props, still renders the same portal into document.body, and the file is 306 lines.

sense_checks.question: Does the ref-based bailout correctly compare all modifier key fields? Does it still fire setState on the first render/mount? No infinite loop risk from ref vs state interaction?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The bailout compares shiftKey, metaKey, ctrlKey, and isMultiSelectModifier individually, preserves the initial mount state through useState, and only mutates a ref before setState so it cannot loop.

sense_checks.question: Do all 11 test files pass (including ClipAction.test.tsx per FLAG-002)? Does git diff show only the files listed in T1-T4 with no out-of-scope edits? Are there zero new files?

meta_commentary: This is a quality/simplification pass — all changes are structural, comment-only, or micro-optimizations. There should be zero behavioral changes from the user's perspective. The biggest risk is T3 (ClipAction extraction) since it moves ~130 lines of JSX into a new component boundary, which could break prop threading or portal behavior. T1-T4 are all independent and can be executed in parallel. T5 is the gate — all 11 test files must pass. FLAG-002 is the one open flag: the plan originally omitted ClipAction.test.tsx from the test list, but it directly tests the menu surface being refactored in T3, so it's been added to T5. The user confirmed three key decisions: serial insertion stays (no Promise.all), registerPeerClear stays as-is, no new test files needed.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Add comment to AgentChat merge preference logic
Step 2: Clean up inline arrow functions in GenerationsPaneGallery
Step 3: Add ordering comment to createShotWithGenerationsPath
Step 4: Remove unused type aliases in shotCreationTypes
Step 6: Extract ClipAction context menu into separate component
Step 7: Add re-render bailout to useModifierKeys
Step 9: Review diff for scope creep

validation.completeness_notes: All 9 plan steps are covered. Steps 1-4 (Phase 1 low-risk cleanups) are batched into T1 since they are all small, independent edits. Steps 8-9 (validation) are batched into T5. FLAG-002 addressed by adding ClipAction.test.tsx to T5's test list.
